decisions.title: Prioritize Python Rules alarming infrastructure before GA
decisions.context: ENG-10201 signals Python Rules needs alarming page. Core capability gap for 'Nail the Core' pillar. Blocks production readiness and customer trust in automated decision workflows.

arguments.for: Alarming is table stakes for production rule systems. Without it, customers can't trust Python Rules for critical decisions. Directly impacts North Star metric (decisions captured).
arguments.against: May delay other Python Rules features. Could scope to MVP alarming and iterate. Resource contention with Canvas and Explore V2 work.

decisions.title: Address ghost events bug (ENG-9978) as P0 - impacts decision reliability
decisions.context: Ghost events with 0s duration triggering unexpectedly on rules with counter >97. Directly undermines 'Signal>Evidence>Decision' strategy if evidence layer is corrupt. Trust erosion risk.

arguments.for: Core product reliability issue. Affects customer trust in decision outputs. Edge case but indicates deeper state management problem. Small customer base amplifies reputational risk.
arguments.against: Appears edge case (counter >97). May be isolated. Other critical path items competing for resources. Could workaround with counter limits.

decisions.title: Consolidate client-side parsing strategy (HDF5 + TDMS) before adding more formats
decisions.context: ENG-9968 and ENG-9980 both implementing client-side parsing for detect_configs. Pattern emerging. Should we standardize approach before Sift Edge work scales?

arguments.for: Building technical debt with one-off parsers. Sift Edge strategy requires scalable ingestion. Standardization now prevents refactor tax later. Supports 'Build the Moat' via extensibility.
arguments.against: May over-engineer for current needs. Customer formats vary widely. Premature abstraction risk. Could ship working solutions faster with tactical approach.

decisions.title: Resource allocation: ROM Prototype (ENG-9250) vs core product debt
decisions.context: ROM prototype active while production issues exist (ghost events, UX gaps, missing permissions UX). Innovation vs. execution tension. Strategy says 'Nail the Core' first.

arguments.for: ROM could be moat-building differentiator. Early prototypes inform architecture. Innovation requires dedicated time. Market window may close.
arguments.against: Core product has reliability and UX gaps. Current customers aren't getting full value. North Star metric needs stable foundation. Premature to expand scope before nailing basics.

decisions.title: Invest in Canvas UX polish vs new features - alignment defaults, beta tags, org switching
decisions.context: ENG-10177 (missing alignment defaults), ENG-9578 (beta tag in wrong place), ENG-3702 (org switching UX). Canvas is 'Embed Deeper' pillar - UX determines adoption depth.

arguments.for: Polish signals product maturity. Small friction points compound to abandonment. Power users need fluid workflows. Quality over features aligns with 'Nail the Core'.
arguments.against: New capabilities may drive more usage than polish. Competitors may leapfrog features. Power users often tolerate friction. Resources limited.

decisions.title: Formalize ingest observability strategy before scaling (ENG-10175, ENG-10193, ENG-10199)
decisions.context: Multiple ingest observability gaps: production load testing gaps, batch age alarm robustness, canary+alarms work. Infrastructure for evidence layer quality.
decisions.blockedBy: Need ingest load testing results and architecture review for alarm consolidation approach

arguments.for: Can't scale evidence layer without observability. Production incidents erode trust. Proactive monitoring prevents customer-facing issues. Enables confident scaling.
arguments.against: Observability is infinite work. Could be over-investing relative to current scale. Reactive fixes may suffice. Resource intensive with uncertain ROI.
